based_on: Structurally derived from the same step 1 receipt design as fixtures/drafts/mazur.golden.draft.jsonl (not yet committed at the time of this fixture's authoring).

what_was_NOT_mutated: optimizer.factors[*].value (product of factors still equals the original gradient)
updates[4].update (still derived from original gradient, so it will cascade to Rule 5 failure)
updates[4].weight_after (still derived from original update, so it will cascade to Rule 6 failure under recomputation)

cascading_failures_expected: Rule 5: update == learning_rate * gradient (cascade; stored update was derived from original gradient, so it is no longer consistent with the mutated stored gradient)
Rule 6: weight_after == weight_before + update (cascade only if the reconciler recomputes update from the mutated gradient; if it uses the stored update value, Rule 6 passes independently)
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures. The post_update_forward.status = 'pending_engine_first_run' on this fixture must NOT mask the Rule 4 failure. If the reconciler reports 'draft not promoted' before reporting Rule 4, the failure-priority rule is violated.
error_message_format_note: The 'delta: 1.000000000e-6' rendering is via src/error-format.ts (scientific notation permitted for error magnitudes), not src/format.ts (receipt emission, plain decimal only). Error-format and receipt-format are separate code paths.
purpose: Proves the verifier checks math, not just byte equality and schema validity. Written and committed BEFORE the engine produces any receipt, so the verifier cannot be co-developed against engine output. Implements 'bad receipts precede good receipts' from the law stack.
post_v0_1_extensions: v0.2 should add one bad fixture per reconciliation rule (target = eight bad fixtures total, one per rule). v0.1 only requires the Rule 4 fixture as the minimum anti-circularity gate.
